records.name: Tabelwaarden selecteren voor berekening

evidence_refs.note: Target exercise asks students to read an ice-cream price/quantity table and interpret a percentage claim.
Cost-structure target requires selecting TK, TVK, TCK, GTK, GVK, and GCK values from source data.

records.pitfall: Student chooses the visible number but not the relevant row, column, base value, or comparison value.

records.name: Waarden aflezen uit staafdiagram

evidence_refs.note: Bar-chart value reading is a common representation-transfer task that cannot be assumed from table reading; no source mutation is proposed in RX.1.

records.pitfall: Student compares bar height visually without checking scale or category labels.

records.name: Waarden aflezen uit lijngrafiek

evidence_refs.role: owned target exercise with CPI over time
evidence_refs.note: Inflation/CPI target exercise requires comparing values across years and avoiding index-point traps.
Line graphs introduce period/point selection and interpolation errors distinct from table reading.

records.pitfall: Student reads the wrong year or treats a period change as a point value.

records.name: Aandelen aflezen uit cirkeldiagram

evidence_refs.note: Pie charts encode shares, not raw absolute quantities; this is a representation distinction worth reviewing before mutation.

records.pitfall: Student treats a share from a pie chart as an absolute number.

records.name: Absolute hoeveelheid berekenen uit aandeel en totaal
records.candidate_unit_name: Absolute hoeveelheid berekenen uit aandeel en totaal

evidence_refs.note: Question asks to show more than 40% from bron 1; source values are not extracted yet, so this remains a reviewed candidate pattern.
Share x total is a separate composed operation from reading the share alone.

records.pitfall: Student multiplies by the wrong total or forgets to convert percentage to decimal/share form.

records.name: Basiswaarde en vergelijkingswaarde in bron bepalen
records.candidate_unit_name: Basiswaarde en vergelijkingswaarde in bron bepalen

evidence_refs.note: Percentage and index exercise includes old/new values and index-point confusion.
Target asks which table interval could produce a 50% drop.

records.pitfall: Student uses the new value as denominator or reverses old and new values.

records.name: Procentuele verandering berekenen vanuit tabel

evidence_refs.note: Target asks students to inspect table values and evaluate a 50% sales drop claim.
Integration target uses mixed source material and table-based real/nominal calculations.

records.pitfall: Student correctly computes percentage change but selects the wrong pair of table values.

records.name: Procentuele verandering berekenen vanuit staafdiagram

evidence_refs.note: Representation transfer from base percentage-change formula to bar charts should be reviewed before mutation; no syllabus-only basis is used.

records.pitfall: Student estimates bar heights without reading the axis scale.

records.name: Procentuele verandering berekenen vanuit lijngrafiek

evidence_refs.note: Book 4 returns to percentage/index interpretation across years.
Time-series values create old/new selection errors distinct from base percentage calculation.

records.pitfall: Student subtracts visually or uses index-point change as percentage change.

records.name: Percentagepuntverandering in aandeel herkennen

evidence_refs.note: Target explicitly asks why index from 108 to 112 is not automatically 4% inflation.
Blueprint repeats the index-point versus percentage distinction in Book 4.

records.pitfall: Student says a rise from 20% to 25% is 5% instead of 5 percentage points.

records.name: Procentuele verandering berekenen vanuit cirkeldiagram

evidence_refs.note: Pie-chart percentage change requires reconstructing absolute values before applying percentage-change logic; hold for human review if evidence remains thin.

records.pitfall: Student compares shares directly even when totals differ across periods.

records.name: Indexcijfer berekenen vanuit tabel

evidence_refs.note: Target asks students to calculate price index from 2023 and 2025 basket values.
Book 4 target uses CPI/index values to calculate real values.

records.pitfall: Student uses the current year instead of base year as 100.

records.name: Indexverandering aflezen uit lijngrafiek

evidence_refs.note: Index series reading is distinct from raw-value line-graph reading because students confuse index-point and percentage changes.
Book 4 repeats the index-point distinction as a critical exam skill.

records.pitfall: Student treats an index-point change as a percentage change.

records.name: Procentuele verandering berekenen vanuit indexcijfers

evidence_refs.note: Target asks for price increase from an index rising to 112 and corrects the 108-to-112 misconception.
Book 4 target applies CPI/index interpretation to real wage and inflation.

records.pitfall: Student subtracts index values instead of applying percentage change from the old index.

records.name: Totale winst berekenen uit opbrengsten- en kostentabel
records.candidate_unit_name: Totale winst berekenen uit opbrengsten- en kostentabel

evidence_refs.note: Target asks students to calculate TO and winst from bakery data.

evidence_refs.role: table with TK TO Winst MK MO
evidence_refs.note: Target explicitly builds a Q/TK/TO/Winst table.

records.pitfall: Student reads the wrong row or mixes total and average values.

records.name: Totale winst berekenen uit P, GTK en Q
records.candidate_unit_name: Totale winst berekenen uit P, GTK en Q

evidence_refs.note: Question asks which area gives total profit for Sprinter.
Producer target requires calculating winst after finding optimal output.

records.pitfall: Student uses TK instead of GTK or forgets to multiply the margin by Q.

records.name: Break-even aflezen uit TO-TK-grafiek

evidence_refs.note: Target asks students to draw TK and TO, mark break-even, and shade profit.

records.pitfall: Student marks the wrong crossing or treats a profit area as the break-even point.

records.name: Winst of verlies aflezen uit TO-TK-grafiek
records.candidate_unit_name: Winst of verlies aflezen uit TO-TK-grafiek

evidence_refs.note: Target asks students to show TK and TO in a graph and identify winst/verlies zones.

records.pitfall: Student reads horizontal distance instead of vertical distance between TO and TK.

records.name: Maximale winst bepalen uit TO-TK-tabel

evidence_refs.note: Target asks students to extend a table and find which Q has highest winst.
Question asks to calculate maximum total profit for Fluid.

records.pitfall: Student selects highest revenue rather than highest profit.

records.name: Winstrechthoek tekenen in producentengrafiek

evidence_refs.note: Question asks which area gives total profit.
Profit maximization target culminates in calculating winst at optimal Q.

records.pitfall: Student draws rectangle to the wrong average-cost line or quantity.

records.name: Winst, verlies en break-even herkennen in producentengrafiek
records.candidate_unit_name: Winst, verlies en break-even herkennen in producentengrafiek

evidence_refs.note: Target asks students to draw long-run equilibrium and show P = minimum GTK = MK.
Producer graph question requires identifying the relevant profit area.

records.pitfall: Student recognises output but not whether P is above, equal to, or below GTK.

records.name: Elasticiteit berekenen uit tabelwaarden

evidence_refs.note: Question asks to explain a table cell using price elasticity.
Elasticity paragraph requires concept and calculation.

records.pitfall: Student computes one percentage change correctly but pairs it with the wrong driver variable.

records.name: Elasticiteit berekenen uit vraaggrafiek

evidence_refs.note: Question asks to derive elasticity from a curve; graph extraction is the bottleneck, even though this case is supply-side.
Price elasticity target requires calculation and interpretation.

records.pitfall: Student reads only one point or swaps P and Q changes.

records.name: Omzetverandering beoordelen met elasticiteit uit bron
records.candidate_unit_name: Omzetverandering beoordelen met elasticiteit uit bron

evidence_refs.note: Question asks to explain how supermarket price rise leads to revenue increase.
Target explicitly connects elastic/inelastic demand to revenue direction.

records.pitfall: Student applies the elasticity sign mechanically without linking it to total revenue direction.

records.name: Welvaartsgebieden herkennen in P-Q diagram

evidence_refs.note: Consumer surplus and welfare graph work is already present in existing A-units.

records.hold_reason: Existing A19/A32/A40 already cover core surplus and welfare-area operations; do not mint before detailed overlap review.
records.pitfall: New unit could duplicate existing welfare diagram operations.

duplicate_overlap.hold_reason: Existing A19/A32/A40 already cover core surplus and welfare-area operations; do not mint before detailed overlap review.

records.name: Beweging langs curve versus verschuiving

evidence_refs.note: Target already requires movement vs shift classification.

records.hold_reason: D32 and A42 already touch this; design review needed before adding another graph-change unit.
records.pitfall: Could fragment an existing conceptual distinction into a redundant A-domain unit.

duplicate_overlap.hold_reason: D32 and A42 already touch this; design review needed before adding another graph-change unit.

records.name: Korte zijde en interventiehoeveelheid

evidence_refs.note: Earlier target flag led to A56 in R4.5; do not re-mint.

records.hold_reason: A51/A56/A59 now cover surplus/shortage and short-side/intervention quantity logic.
records.pitfall: Would duplicate recently minted R4.5 units.

duplicate_overlap.hold_reason: A51/A56/A59 now cover surplus/shortage and short-side/intervention quantity logic.

records.name: Marginale waarden uit tabel

evidence_refs.note: Target led to A52 in R4.5.

records.hold_reason: A52 already covers MK and MO from table differences.
records.pitfall: Would duplicate A52.

duplicate_overlap.hold_reason: A52 already covers MK and MO from table differences.

records.name: Grafische MO=MK optimum bepalen

evidence_refs.note: Target uses MO=MK and producer graph reasoning.
Monopoly procedure may already encode the operation.

records.hold_reason: May overlap existing profit optimization and verification units; needs human review before minting.
records.pitfall: Could conflate graphical optimum reading with algebraic MO=MK solving.

duplicate_overlap.hold_reason: May overlap existing profit optimization and verification units; needs human review before minting.
